Notes.Text:
This update for perl-libwww-perl fixes the following issue

- CVE-2026-8368: LWP: UserAgent: Authorization and Proxy-Authorization headers are leaked on cross-origin redirects
  (bsc#1265156).
Notes.Title: Details

Notes.Text: The CVRF data is provided by SUSE under the Creative Commons License 4.0 with Attribution (CC-BY-4.0).
Notes.Title: Terms of Use

Relationships.RelatesToProductReference: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server LTSS Extended Security 12 SP5

Vulnerabilities.Description:
LWP::UserAgent versions before 6.83 for Perl leak Authorization and Proxy-Authorization headers on cross-origin redirects.

On a 3xx response, the redirect handler strips only Host and Cookie before issuing the follow-up request. Caller-supplied Authorization and Proxy-Authorization headers are sent unchanged to the redirect target, including across scheme, host, or port changes.

A redirect to an attacker controlled host therefore discloses the caller's credentials to that host.

ProductID: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server LTSS Extended Security 12 SP5:perl-libwww-perl-6.05-6.3.1
